title: Grinding sumi ink on an inkstone
sources: Broadly documented in pre-Meiji calligraphy primers — general knowledge of the East-Asian writing tradition; no single Western PD canonical source
notes: Grinding ink on a suzuri. Covers: how much water to start with, the slow circular motion and angle of the inkstick, testing for the right density (not too watery, not so thick it beads on the stone). The ink-stone well holds the pooled ink; the grinding platform is where the stick works. Bottled sumi is acceptable for practice but grinding provides a meditative warm-up and a denser, warmer result.
